text:
Once upon a time in a small town, there sat an old oak tree. It had seen many winters, and it watched over every child who grew up playing under its big branches.

There was Lily. She was small but brave. Every weekend she would gather acorns then fill a jar with them. She liked to think of them as treasures.

One autumn, as Lily prepared for her little gathering, she heard tales about a magical creature—a wise old owl that lived high in the tree. There were secrets it knew.

Lily climbed higher. That's where the owl lived. But it seemed quiet this year.

"Poor thing," Lily murmured. "Maybe it just wants some food."

She flew down to the tree trunk. There sat the owl—old, wise, with feathers as dark as pitch.

"Lily," it said softly. "You're going to collect more than acorns."

*"What do you mean?*" she asked curiously.

*"Find something that understands you
